purpose: Criterion × pageType × business_type applicability matrix. Layer 3 of doctrine (after scope classification, after synergy compensation). Declares when a criterion is APPLICABLE, N/A, or weighted differently. Does NOT override exclusions already in page_type_criteria.json — augments them.

ecommerce.description: Transactional ecommerce — conversion = purchase. Includes DTC, beauty, fashion, food, home, wellness, entertainment.

lead_gen.description: Lead generation — conversion = form submission / demo booking. Includes services, insurance, B2B non-SaaS, and many LPs.

saas.description: Software-as-a-service — conversion = signup / trial / demo. Includes B2B SaaS, fintech SaaS.

content.description: Content / media / learning. Conversion = article read, email signup, engagement.

app.description: Mobile / consumer apps — conversion = app install, onboarding start.

applicability_statuses.OPTIONAL: May score; N/A if absent (don't penalize).
applicability_statuses.NA: Criterion does not apply; excluded from rawMax.
applicability_statuses.BONUS: Doesn't count in rawMax, but score added as bonus (+X% up to cap).

applicability_rules.description: All entries already in playbook/page_type_criteria.json.pageTypeSpecs.[pt].universalExclusions remain NA and are honored first.
Social proof (per_04, per_05) and risk reversal (psy_04) are REQUIRED on ecommerce PDPs and checkouts.
Lead gen forms must have a clear risk reversal (no spam, GDPR, unsubscribe).
SaaS pages must hit scent trail + positioning cleanly — value prop coherence is table stakes.

Content/editorial pages (blogs, listicles, advertorials, comparisons) should NOT use urgency or scarcity tactics — anti-brand pour SaaS premium type Weglot/Linear/Stripe blog. Étendu V3.2.1 (Sprint 2.5 V26.AA) : ajout psy_02 + advertorial/comparison.
App marketing relies on hero → install. Hero quality is mission-critical.
Thank-you pages are post-conversion; most persuasion criteria N/A, tech + coherence still apply.
Checkout pages must display trust signals (psy_05), security (tech_02), and clear totals. Everything else N/A.
Blog articles must be scannable and readable on mobile — ux_01 + ux_05 REQUIRED.
VSL / video pages: hero_05 (visual) evaluates the video thumbnail — bonus-weighted.
Squeeze pages: only hero + single CTA + simple form matter. Most criteria N/A.

lp.rationale: Legacy 'lp' pageType used by emma_matelas, monday, revolut, stripe. Inspection shows they are all lead-gen-style landing pages. Alias to lp_leadgen resolves 8 batch failures without data migration.

implementation_notes: The aliases + rules are consumed at runtime by page_type_filter.py + score_contextual_overlay.py + score_page_type.py.
page_type_filter must treat alias pageTypes transparently (lp → lp_leadgen lookup).
Applicability status REQUIRED doubles the penalty when score=0 (applied at aggregation).
Status BONUS removes criterion from rawMax and adds score as extra points (capped at +5% of pillar max).
Status NA removes criterion entirely from kept_criteria (same mechanism as existing universalExclusions).
Weight bias is applied to the final pillar score100 in aggregate, so business-type specialization affects overall ranking without corrupting per-pillar interpretability.
Audit trace: every applied rule logs {rule_id, applicability, before, after} in score_page_type.json.applicability_overlay.
